figures.caption: Fig. 1. Overview of our visuo-tactile data collection system. (a) The hardware design, featuring direct-drive finger grips for haptic feedback, tactile sensors, a camera, and an annotation button. (b) Sample tactile readings from the left (L) and right (R) sensors during contact with different objects. The white boundary outlines the entire object, while the magenta overlay indicates the part occluded by the gripper. (c) An example image sequence of a demonstration captured by the attached camera. We crop the images for better visualization and highlight the task objects in red and purple colors. We use the annotation button to label each segment as coarse or fine; we then visualize the resulting segments.

Fig. 2. Single-demonstration summary of the toy insertion task collected with our device: (a) gripper position trajectory (b) gripper width over time (c) images and tactile measurements of event frames.
